Functions for creating, handling, and destroying health activity summary layers in a UI.

# Purpose
This C header file defines an interface for managing a health activity summary card within a user interface. It includes function declarations for creating, handling, and destroying a special UI layer that displays health data. The function [`health_activity_summary_card_create`](<#health_activity_summary_card_create>) initializes a new layer with the provided health data. The [`health_activity_summary_card_select_click_handler`](<#health_activity_summary_card_select_click_handler>) manages user interactions with the layer, while [`health_activity_summary_card_destroy`](<#health_activity_summary_card_destroy>) deallocates the layer when it is no longer needed. Additionally, the file provides functions to retrieve the background color of the layer with [`health_activity_summary_card_get_bg_color`](<#health_activity_summary_card_get_bg_color>) and to check if a select indicator is available with [`health_activity_summary_show_select_indicator`](<#health_activity_summary_show_select_indicator>). The file includes necessary dependencies from `health_data.h` and `applib/ui/ui.h`.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `health_data.h`
- `applib/ui/ui.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### health\_activity\_summary\_card\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_activity_summary_card_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_activity_summary_card.h#L31>)

Creates a layer with health activity summary data.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new layer that displays a health activity summary. This function initializes the layer with the provided health data and sets up necessary visual components, such as an icon and a progress bar. Ensure that the `health_data` parameter is valid and properly initialized before calling this function. The caller is responsible for managing the memory of the returned layer, including its eventual destruction.
- **Inputs**:
    - `health_data`: A pointer to a `HealthData` structure containing the health data to display. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the `HealthData`.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a newly allocated `Layer` that contains its own data, initialized with the provided health data.
- **See Also**: [`health_activity_summary_card_create`](<health_activity_summary_card.c.md#health_activity_summary_card_create>)  (Implementation)

### health\_activity\_summary\_card\_select\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_activity_summary_card_select_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_activity_summary_card.h#L35>)

Handles the select click event for a health activity summary card layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to handle the select click event on a health activity summary card layer. It retrieves the health data associated with the layer and creates a new window to display detailed health activity information. This function must be called with a valid layer that contains its own data, as it relies on this data to function correctly. Ensure that the layer is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `layer`: A pointer to an existing `Layer` that contains its own data. The layer must not be null and should be properly initialized with health activity summary data. If the layer is invalid or uninitialized, the behavior is undefined.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_activity_summary_card_select_click_handler`](<health_activity_summary_card.c.md#health_activity_summary_card_select_click_handler>)  (Implementation)


---
### health\_activity\_summary\_card\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_activity_summary_card_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_activity_summary_card.h#L39>)

Destroy a special layer and free its resources.
- **Description**: Use this function to release all resources associated with a special layer created for health activity summaries. Call this function when the layer is no longer needed to prevent memory leaks. Ensure that the `base_layer` parameter is a valid pointer to a layer that was previously created and has not already been destroyed.
- **Inputs**:
    - `base_layer`: A pointer to an existing layer containing its own data. Must not be null and must point to a valid layer that has not been destroyed yet. The function will handle invalid pointers by not performing any operations.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`health_activity_summary_card_destroy`](<health_activity_summary_card.c.md#health_activity_summary_card_destroy>)  (Implementation)

### health\_activity\_summary\_card\_get\_bg\_color<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_activity_summary_card_get_bg_color}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_activity_summary_card.h#L42>)

Gets the background color of a health activity summary card layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the background color of a health activity summary card layer. This function does not modify the layer or any other data. It is useful when you need to know the current background color for display or comparison purposes. Ensure that the `layer` parameter is a valid pointer to a layer that represents a health activity summary card.
- **Inputs**:
    - `layer`: A pointer to a `Layer` object. This must be a valid layer that represents a health activity summary card. The function does not modify the layer, and the caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: Returns a `GColor` value representing the background color of the specified layer.
- **See Also**: [`health_activity_summary_card_get_bg_color`](<health_activity_summary_card.c.md#health_activity_summary_card_get_bg_color>)  (Implementation)

### health\_activity\_summary\_show\_select\_indicator<!-- {{#callable_declaration:health_activity_summary_show_select_indicator}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_activity_summary_card.h#L45>)

Indicates if the select indicator is available for a layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to check if the select indicator is available for a given layer. This function always returns true, indicating that the select indicator is available. It is useful when you need to confirm the availability of the select indicator for a layer in the health activity summary context.
- **Inputs**:
    - `layer`: A pointer to an existing `Layer` object. The `Layer` must contain its own data and must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the `Layer`.
- **Output**: A boolean value `true`, indicating that the select indicator is available for the specified layer.
- **See Also**: [`health_activity_summary_show_select_indicator`](<health_activity_summary_card.c.md#health_activity_summary_show_select_indicator>)  (Implementation)
